relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul returns them to the good news they received and stand in.
The word saves/heals as it is held fast.
The first tradition is Messiah dying for misalignment according to Scripture.
Burial and third-day raising complete the received pattern.
The risen one appears to Cephas and the Twelve.
Hundreds of siblings become resurrection witnesses.
James and all apostles receive appearance.
Paul receives the appearance as one untimely born.
He remembers pursuing the assembly and calls himself least.
Grace makes him what he is and labors through him.
The shared proclamation is the trust they received.
Messiah’s resurrection challenges denial of resurrection.
No resurrection of dead ones would empty Messiah’s raising.
Without resurrection, proclamation and trust are empty.
Witness itself would become false.
If dead ones are not raised, Messiah is not raised.
Without Messiah raised, trust is futile and misalignment remains.
Those sleeping in Messiah would be lost.
Hope only for this life would be pitiable.
But Messiah is raised as firstfruit of those sleeping.
Through human came death; through human comes resurrection.
In Adam all die; in Messiah all are made alive.
Each comes in order: firstfruit, then those of Messiah.
The completion comes when every ruling power is made inactive.
Messiah reigns until enemies are underfoot.
Death is the last enemy made inactive.
All things are subjected beneath him, except the One subjecting all.
The final horizon is the Divine all in all.
Practices around the dead make no sense if the dead are not raised.
Paul’s hourly danger assumes resurrection hope.
He dies daily inside the Messiah boast.
Without resurrection, only eat, drink, and death remain.
Harmful companionship corrupts good character.
Sober justice and knowledge of the Divine are needed.
The question becomes: what body rises?
Seed must die to be made alive.
What is sown is not yet the body that will be.
The Divine gives each seed its own body.
Flesh itself has varieties.
Heavenly and earthly bodies carry different glory.
Sun, moon, and stars differ in glory.
Resurrection raises decay into incorruptibility.
Dishonor rises into glory; weakness rises into power.
The soul-level body rises as Spirit-body.
The last Adam becomes life-making Spirit.
Natural comes first, then spiritual.
Dust-human and heaven-human name two origins.
Dust-bearing and heaven-bearing humanity mirror their source.
As we bore dust-image, we will bear heaven-image.
Decay cannot inherit incorruptibility.
The mystery is change, not disembodied escape.
At the trumpet, the dead are raised incorruptible and we are changed.
The decaying puts on incorruptibility; the dying puts on deathlessness.
Death is swallowed into victory.
Death loses victory and sting.
Misalignment gives death its sting, and Law gives misalignment its power.
Thanks to the Divine who gives victory through Jesus Messiah.
Because resurrection is real, beloved ones can be steadfast and know labor is not empty.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 15 is embodied cosmic hope. The spiritual body is still body: transformed, incorruptible, deathless, and held in the promise that labor in the Lord is not empty.
